_comment: Trip essentials shown in the Planning section on the homepage.

essentials.title: Rest & Readiness
essentials.description: Get enough sleep before the hike or ride, eat properly, and start only when your body feels ready for the route.

essentials.title: Food & Water
essentials.description: Enough water, electrolytes, trail snacks, emergency calories, and a plan for refilling if possible.

essentials.title: First Aid
essentials.description: Bandages, antiseptic wipes, blister care, pain relief, and any personal medication you may need.

essentials.title: Navigation
essentials.description: Save the route offline, bring a map or backup navigation, and tell someone your planned route and expected return time.

essentials.title: Repair & Weather
essentials.description: Basic tools, spare tube or patch kit, rain layer, sun protection, and clothing for changing conditions.

essentials.title: Power
essentials.description: Extra batteries, charged phone, lights, GPS or watch power, and a small power bank for longer days.

essentials.title: Overnight Kit
essentials.description: For overnight trips, bring a tent or shelter, sleeping gear, extra warm layers, a headlamp, and enough food and water for the next day.

essentials.title: Emergency Info
essentials.description: Carry identification, emergency contact details, a little cash, and any important medical information in an easy-to-find place.
